description: desegregation and voters' rights coalition formed in Albany, Georgia
description_wiki: The Albany Movement was a desegregation and voters' rights coalition formed in Albany, Georgia, in November 1961.

description: public university located in Albany, Georgia, United States
description_wiki: Albany State University is a public historically black university in Albany, Georgia, United States.

description: city in and county seat of Dougherty County, Georgia, United States
description_wiki: Albany ( awl-BIN-ee) is a city in the U.S. state of Georgia.

aliases: National Association for the Advancement of Colored People
description: civil rights organization in the United States

aliases: National League on Urban Conditions Among Negroes
description_wiki: The National Urban League (NUL), formerly known as the National League on Urban Conditions Among Negroes, is a nonpartisan historic civil rights organization based in New York City that advocates on behalf of economic and social justice for African Americans and against racial discrimination in the United States.

description: American Baptist minister and civil rights leader (1929–1968)
American civil rights activist and minister (1926–1990)
American businesswoman known for civil rights activism
description_wiki: Diane Judith Nash (born May 15, 1938) is an American civil rights activist and strategist of the student wing of the Civil Rights Movement.

description: state of the United States of America
description_wiki: Tennessee ( , locally ), officially the State of Tennessee, is a landlocked state in the Southeastern region of the United States.

description: neighborhood in Manhattan, New York City, New York, United States
description_wiki: Harlem is a neighborhood in Upper Manhattan, New York City.

description: chief of police of Albany, Georgia during the Albany Movement (1926–2000)
description_wiki: Laurie Pritchett (December 9, 1926 – November 13, 2000) was city Chief of Police in Albany, Georgia, best known for his actions in 1961 and 1962 suppressing the city's civil rights demonstrations by the Albany Movement.

description: reserve force of the United States Army and Air Force
description_wiki: The National Guard is a military reserve organization of the United States Department of Defense (DoD).

description: largest student-led civil rights organization during the American Civil Rights Movement
description_wiki: The Student Nonviolent Coordinating Committee, and later the Student National Coordinating Committee (SNCC, pronounced  SNIK), was the principal channel of student commitment in the United States to the civil rights movement during the 1960s.
Bernice Johnson Reagon (October 4, 1942 – July 16, 2024) was an American song leader, composer, professor of American history, curator at the Smithsonian, and social activist.

aliases: Meet the Press with Chuck Todd
Meet the Press with Kristen Welker
description: American television Sunday morning talk show broadcast on NBC
description_wiki: Meet the Press also known as Meet the Press with Kristen Welker is a weekly American television Sunday morning talk show broadcast on NBC. It is the longest-running program on American television, though its format has changed since the debut episode on November 6, 1947.
